concerns.text: FR-001 mandates support for '≥ 10,000 stored episodes' with specific latency constraints. The tasks list (T001-T037) includes general setup and implementation of the EpisodicMemory class (T012) and retrieval service (T014), but lacks a specific task to validate the system's performance, memory usage, or latency at the 10,000-episode scale. T011 only tests with 1,000 entries. Without a task to verify the 10k scale, the FR-001 requirement is untestable.

SC-004 and US-3 require measuring 'Scene construction coherence' via 'human evaluation ratings'. Task T030 implements a 'human evaluation interface script'. However, the Constitution (Principle I) and Plan mandate that results must be reproducible by re-running code on a fresh runner. A script alone does not constitute a reproducible task for human evaluation unless it includes a mechanism for synthetic proxy scoring or integration with an external service. There is no task to define or implement this reproducibility mechanism, leaving the SC-004 metric unachievable in the CI environment.

Task T032 description includes specific named reviewers ('Turing, Kandel, Kahneman, Krakauer'). This violates the project instruction to never copy bracketed attribution markers or specific persona names into project artifacts. These should be integrated as plain prose or addressed in the content rather than listed as task metadata.
